mission.title: Install and Configure Continuous Optimization on Kubernetes
mission.description: Krkn is a chaos and resiliency testing tool for Kubernetes that helps improve performance under failure conditions. By injecting deliberate failures into your Kubernetes clusters, it allows you to assess and enhance their resilience.

steps.title: Install Krkn (Kraken) via pip
steps.description:
Install the Krkn chaos testing tool:
```bash
pip install krkn
```
Or run via container:
```bash
docker pull quay.io/redhat-chaos/krkn:latest
```

steps.title: Configure a chaos scenario
steps.description:
Create a configuration file:
```bash
cat > config.yaml << 'EOF'
kraken:
  kubeconfig_path: ~/.kube/config
  scenarios:
    - pod_scenarios/pod-delete.yaml
  litmus_scenarios: []
  exit_on_failure: false
EOF
```

steps.title: Run a chaos test
steps.description:
Execute a chaos scenario:
```bash
krkn --config config.yaml
```
Or via Docker:
```bash
docker run --rm -v ~/.kube:/root/.kube quay.io/redhat-chaos/krkn:latest --config config.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Scenario fails with RBAC errors
troubleshooting.description: Krkn needs cluster-admin permissions to inject chaos. Verify your kubeconfig has admin access.

troubleshooting.title: Pod not being killed
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod scenario config matches existing pod labels and namespaces.

resolution.summary: Krkn is installed for running chaos engineering tests against Kubernetes clusters.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+ required. kubectl and a running cluster needed for chaos testing.
